sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.44 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: (P.\,Hall). Is there a non-trivial group which is isomorphic with every proper extension of itself by itself? { J.\,C.\,Lennox} Yes, such groups exist of cardinality any regular cardinal (R.\,G\"obel, S.\,Shelah, Math. Proc. Cambridge Philos. Society (2), { 134}, no.\,1 (2003), 23--31).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
